behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: The core failure is at a local module boundary (diff-status parsing/normalization -> report aggregation). No real OS/network/subprocess behavior is required if the test feeds realistic `git --name-status` rename lines (e.g., `R100\told\tnew`) through the parser and aggregator together. Rejected System because the behavior text is about category accounting, not subprocess/OS semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser + classifier + report builder in-process. Feed fixture diff-status text containing renames and verify output has rename counts only, with add/delete unchanged for those entries.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary handling failures often come from real git subprocess output/encoding and file content interactions (e.g., decode errors, unexpected binary markers). In-process mocks can hide these crash paths, so real repo + real git invocation is the minimum to catch true failures.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp git repo with a real binary file change, run the CLI as a subprocess, assert zero crash/non-error exit and that binary-related changes are reflected in the structured stdout report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real git behavior for unborn HEAD/no-commit repos (exit codes/stderr semantics). Mocking git calls can trivially pass and miss real failure handling. Therefore real subprocess + real repo state is required.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh git repo with no commits, execute the CLI subprocess, assert it exits successfully and prints an empty structured report instead of propagating git errors.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against actual git history. Real failures emerge from interactions among history traversal, diff selection, parsing, and counting over real commits. A mocked history source can hide mismatches between implemented git commands and true repository state.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a known commit sequence containing adds/deletes/renames/modifications, run the CLI, and compare reported counts to expected counts derived from the constructed history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics are git index/worktree behaviors exposed via real commands. In-process fakes of git status/diff can miss command-selection and flag mistakes that only appear with a real index. No AI dependency, single-step CLI check, so System (not Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, produce equivalent file changes in two scenarios (staged vs unstaged), run the CLI subprocess for each, and assert identical structured stdout output.
